text.title: ProductionEvaluator — Continuous Production Monitoring
text.summary: Sampled production evaluation with automated quality checks, alert thresholds, and persistent JSONL logging instead of in-memory accumulation.

rule.statement: Set sample rate based on traffic volume: 10% at 1k req/day, 1% at 100k req/day, 0.1% at 1M req/day.
rule.rationale: Evaluation should cost less than 5% of production inference cost; higher traffic requires lower sample rates to stay within budget.

rule.statement: Log all raw production requests separately from the evaluation sample.
rule.rationale: random.random() sampling is not reproducible; when debugging a specific failure, the sample cannot reliably re-capture it without a separate raw log.

rule.statement: Calibrate error pattern strings (e.g., "sorry, i cannot", "as an ai") against actual model outputs every 3-6 months.
rule.rationale: Provider phrasings change across model versions; stale patterns produce false negatives that mask real quality issues.

antipattern.description: Storing all evaluation logs in memory with `self.evaluation_log.append(evaluation)` in a long-running process.
antipattern.reason: Unbounded list growth causes OOM in high-volume systems; flush to a persistent store (JSONL file, database) periodically.

example.note: Appends to JSONL file per request — bounded memory, persistent across restarts.

rule.statement: Only invoke LLMJudge on sampled production requests when sample_rate is below 5% — the boolean check must use the actual configured rate, not a hardcoded constant.
rule.rationale: The original ProductionEvaluator has a bug: it checks `self.config.sample_rate < 0.05` but at 5% sample rate this condition is false and judge scores are never computed.
